title: salix exigua
intro: Salix exigua, also known as the coyote willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae). The bark and leaves of the coyote willow contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which makes it useful for medicinal purposes. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of Salix exigua include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
